channel.title: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!
channel.description:
So after couple months learning React and Frontity, made some project as exercise, finally i able to offer this React-Frontity-WordPress approach for a client and they love it. https://avoblog.demoapp.xyz/ It's still at staging phase. I know it's just a blog website so yeah it's not that complicated. But i really love the process. Everything at that site are manageable at the WordPress backend http://prntscr.com/1xd5xpz . It's also able to sort page section, hide/show etc. Thanks to the power of CMB2.
Thank you guys.

item.title: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!
item.dc:creator: victor.diaz
item.source: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!

item.title: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!
item.dc:creator: gideonjb
item.description:
This looks really clean. I like it a lot.
What theme are you using with Wordpress?
And, if you don’t mind me asking, which tool did you use to build the menu? Is it the same one you show in the screenshot? I’m not familiar with it.

Read full topic
item.source: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!

item.title: Finally, a real project for a client with frontity!
item.dc:creator: qbonszone
item.description:
So after couple months learning React and Frontity, made some project as exercise, finally i able to offer this React-Frontity-WordPress approach for a client and they love it. https://avoblog.demoapp.xyz/ It’s still at staging phase. I know it’s just a blog website so yeah it’s not that complicated. But i really love the process. Everything at that site are manageable at the WordPress backend Screenshot by Lightshot . It’s also able to sort page section, hide/show etc. Thanks to the power of CMB2.

Thank you guys.
